name: Multi-Turn Fabrication Detection
summary: All 6 planning turns successfully triggered create_itinerary_items. Zero fabrication detected — every turn where the AI claimed to have added items actually called the tool. Final itinerary: 38 items across 14 days covering Lima, Sacred Valley, Machu Picchu, Cusco, and Lake Titicaca. The AI proactively filled gaps (Lima Days 2-3, Day 14 departure) in the summary turn without being asked.

ux_issues.title: Duplicate tool calls waste tokens and confuse stream output
ux_issues.description: In 5 of 6 turns, the AI called create_itinerary_items a second time with identical items after already receiving a successful result. The second call returns 'already_exists' / 'skipped_count'. This doubles the tool loop iterations and adds unnecessary latency. The AI seems to re-invoke the tool after generating text, possibly due to the tool loop continuation logic.

ux_issues.title: Duplicate Sillustani Funeral Towers item on Day 12
ux_issues.description: Day 12 contains 'Sillustani Funeral Towers (Moved)' from earlier turns AND Day 14 has 'Sillustani Funeral Towers (Final Stop)'. The Moved version on Day 12 should have been deleted when the AI added the Final Stop to Day 14 for a cleaner itinerary. Minor planning inconsistency.

ux_issues.title: Day 8 acclimatization misplaced after Sacred Valley + Machu Picchu
ux_issues.description: The AI placed 'Acclimatization & Plaza de Armas Stroll' on Day 8, but by that point the traveler has already spent 4 days in the Sacred Valley (2,800-3,000m) and visited Machu Picchu. Acclimatization should have been on the first day at altitude (Day 4). The AI's chronological logic for altitude adaptation was imperfect.

ai_behavior.description: AI consistently called create_itinerary_items on every turn. Zero fabrication across 6 turns.
AI proactively filled content gaps in Turn 6 (summary): added Lima Days 2-3 and Day 14 departure items without being explicitly asked, showing good initiative.
AI used delete_itinerary_items in Turns 1 and 2 to reorganize existing items before adding new ones, showing awareness of the existing itinerary state.
Expert persona handoff to 'Inti' (Peru specialist) happened automatically in selection mode.
Duplicate tool calls on 5/6 turns: AI calls create_itinerary_items, gets result, generates text, then calls it again with the same items. Second call always returns already_exists.
Turn 1 made a web_search call that returned 'no_web_access' — the AI should know from context that web search is unavailable in this environment and not waste a tool iteration.

assertions.turn_3_no_overwrite: PASS — 27 items after turn 3 > 23 items after turn 2
assertions.no_fabrication_detected: PASS — all 6 turns had tool calls backing claims

usefulness.notes: Excellent itinerary with rich detail and good research focus. Deducted 1 point for duplicate tool calls and the acclimatization placement error (Day 8 should be Day 4). The AI's proactive gap-filling in Turn 6 was impressive.
